NKJV.1: Oh, give thanks to the LORD, for He is good! For His mercy endures forever.
NKJV.2: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, Whom He has redeemed from the hand of the enemy,
NKJV.3: And gathered out of the lands, From the east and from the west, From the north and from the south.
NKJV.4: They wandered in the wilderness in a desolate way; They found no city to dwell in.
NKJV.5: Hungry and thirsty, Their soul fainted in them.
NKJV.6: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble, And He delivered them out of their distresses.
NKJV.7: And He led them forth by the right way, That they might go to a city for a dwelling place.
NKJV.8: Oh, that men would give thanks to the LORD for His goodness, And for His wonderful works to the children of men!
NKJV.9: For He satisfies the longing soul, And fills the hungry soul with goodness.
NKJV.10: Those who sat in darkness and in the shadow of death,  Bound in affliction and irons—
NKJV.11: Because they rebelled against the words of God, And despised the counsel of the Most High,
NKJV.12: Therefore He brought down their heart with labor; They fell down, and there was none to help.
NKJV.13: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble, And He saved them out of their distresses.
NKJV.14: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, And broke their chains in pieces.
NKJV.15: Oh, that men would give thanks to the LORD for His goodness, And for His wonderful works to the children of men!
NKJV.16: For He has broken the gates of bronze, And cut the bars of iron in two.
NKJV.17: Fools, because of their transgression, And because of their iniquities, were afflicted.
NKJV.18: Their soul abhorred all manner of food, And they drew near to the gates of death.
NKJV.19: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble, And He saved them out of their distresses.
NKJV.20: He sent His word and healed them, And delivered them from their destructions.
NKJV.21: Oh, that men would give thanks to the LORD for His goodness, And for His wonderful works to the children of men!
NKJV.22: Let them sacrifice the sacrifices of thanksgiving, And declare His works with rejoicing.
NKJV.23: Those who go down to the sea in ships, Who do business on great waters,
NKJV.24: They see the works of the LORD, And His wonders in the deep.
NKJV.25: For He commands and raises the stormy wind, Which lifts up the waves of the sea.
NKJV.26: They mount up to the heavens, They go down again to the depths;  Their soul melts because of trouble.
NKJV.27: They reel to and fro, and stagger like a drunken man, And are at their wits’ end.
NKJV.28: Then they cry out to the LORD in their trouble, And He brings them out of their distresses.
NKJV.29: He calms the storm, So that its waves are still.
NKJV.30: Then they are glad because they are quiet; So He guides them to their desired haven.
NKJV.31: Oh, that men would give thanks to the LORD for His goodness, And for His wonderful works to the children of men!
NKJV.32: Let them exalt Him also in the assembly of the people, And praise Him in the company of the elders.
NKJV.33: He turns rivers into a wilderness, And the watersprings into dry ground;
NKJV.34: A fruitful land into barrenness, For the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
NKJV.35: He turns a wilderness into pools of water, And dry land into watersprings.
NKJV.36: There He makes the hungry dwell, That they may establish a city for a dwelling place,
NKJV.37: And sow fields and plant vineyards, That they may yield a fruitful harvest.
NKJV.38: He also blesses them, and they multiply greatly; And He does not let their cattle decrease.
NKJV.39: When they are diminished and brought low Through oppression, affliction, and sorrow,
NKJV.40: He pours contempt on princes, And causes them to wander in the wilderness where there is no way;
NKJV.41: Yet He sets the poor on high, far from affliction, And makes their families like a flock.
NKJV.42: The righteous see it and rejoice, And all iniquity stops its mouth.
NKJV.43: Whoever is wise will observe these things, And they will understand the lovingkindness of the LORD.

KJV.1: O give thanks<S>3034</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S>, for he is good<S>2896</S>: for his mercy<S>2617</S> endureth for ever<S>5769</S>.
KJV.2: Let the redeemed<S>1350</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S> say<S>559</S> so, whom he hath redeemed<S>1350</S> from the hand<S>3027</S> of the enemy<S>6862</S>;
KJV.3: And gathered<S>6908</S> them out of the lands<S>776</S>, from the east<S>4217</S>, and from the west<S>4628</S>, from the north<S>6828</S>, and from the south<S>3220</S>. <sup>south: Heb. sea</sup>

KJV.18: Their soul<S>5315</S> abhorreth<S>8581</S> all manner of meat<S>400</S>; and they draw near<S>5060</S> unto the gates<S>8179</S> of death<S>4194</S>.
KJV.19: Then they cry<S>2199</S> unto the LORD<S>3068</S> in their trouble<S>6862</S>, and he saveth<S>3467</S> them out of their distresses<S>4691</S>.

KJV.38: He blesseth<S>1288</S> them also, so that they are multiplied<S>7235</S> greatly<S>3966</S>; and suffereth<S>4591</S> not their cattle<S>929</S> to decrease<S>4591</S>.

KJV.41: Yet setteth he the poor<S>34</S> on high<S>7682</S> from affliction<S>6040</S>, and maketh<S>7760</S> him families<S>4940</S> like a flock<S>6629</S>. <sup>from: or, after</sup>

KJV.43: Whoso is wise<S>2450</S>, and will observe<S>8104</S> these things, even they shall understand<S>995</S> the lovingkindness<S>2617</S> of the LORD<S>3068</S>.

ESV.1: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, for his steadfast love endures forever!
ESV.2: Let the redeemed of the LORD say so, whom he has redeemed from trouble
ESV.3: and gathered in from the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.
ESV.4: Some wandered in desert wastes, finding no way to a city to dwell in;
ESV.5: hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted within them.
ESV.6: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
ESV.7: He led them by a straight way till they reached a city to dwell in.
ESV.8: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
ESV.9: For he satisfies the longing soul, and the hungry soul he fills with good things.
ESV.10: Some sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, prisoners in affliction and in irons,
ESV.11: for they had rebelled against the words of God, and spurned the counsel of the Most High.
ESV.12: So he bowed their hearts down with hard labor; they fell down, with none to help.
ESV.13: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
ESV.14: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and burst their bonds apart.
ESV.15: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
ESV.16: For he shatters the doors of bronze and cuts in two the bars of iron.
ESV.17: Some were fools through their sinful ways, and because of their iniquities suffered affliction;
ESV.18: they loathed any kind of food, and they drew near to the gates of death.
ESV.19: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
ESV.20: He sent out his word and healed them, and delivered them from their destruction.
ESV.21: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
ESV.22: And let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving, and tell of his deeds in songs of joy!
ESV.23: Some went down to the sea in ships, doing business on the great waters;
ESV.24: they saw the deeds of the LORD, his wondrous works in the deep.
ESV.25: For he commanded and raised the stormy wind, which lifted up the waves of the sea.
ESV.26: They mounted up to heaven; they went down to the depths; their courage melted away in their evil plight;
ESV.27: they reeled and staggered like drunken men and were at their wits' end.
ESV.28: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, and he delivered them from their distress.
ESV.29: He made the storm be still, and the waves of the sea were hushed.
ESV.30: Then they were glad that the waters  were quiet, and he brought them to their desired haven.
ESV.31: Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love, for his wondrous works to the children of man!
ESV.32: Let them extol him in the congregation of the people, and praise him in the assembly of the elders.
ESV.33: He turns rivers into a desert, springs of water into thirsty ground,
ESV.34: a fruitful land into a salty waste, because of the evil of its inhabitants.
ESV.35: He turns a desert into pools of water, a parched land into springs of water.
ESV.36: And there he lets the hungry dwell, and they establish a city to live in;
ESV.37: they sow fields and plant vineyards and get a fruitful yield.
ESV.38: By his blessing they multiply greatly, and he does not let their livestock diminish.
ESV.39: When they are diminished and brought low through oppression, evil, and sorrow,
ESV.40: he pours contempt on princes and makes them wander in trackless wastes;
ESV.41: but he raises up the needy out of affliction and makes their families like flocks.
ESV.42: The upright see it and are glad, and all wickedness shuts its mouth.
ESV.43: Whoever is wise, let him attend to these things; let them consider the steadfast love of the LORD.

NASB.1: Oh give thanks to the LORD, for He is good, For His lovingkindness is everlasting.
NASB.2: Let the redeemed of the LORD say [so], Whom He has redeemed from the hand of the adversary
NASB.3: And gathered from the lands, From the east and from the west, From the north and from the south.
NASB.4: They wandered in the wilderness in a desert region; They did not find a way to an inhabited city.
NASB.5: [They were] hungry and thirsty; Their soul fainted within them.
NASB.6: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He delivered them out of their distresses.
NASB.7: He led them also by a straight way, To go to an inhabited city.
NASB.8: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
NASB.9: For He has satisfied the thirsty soul, And the hungry soul He has filled with what is good.
NASB.10: There were those who dwelt in darkness and in the shadow of death, Prisoners in misery and chains,
NASB.11: Because they had rebelled against the words of God And spurned the counsel of the Most High.
NASB.12: Therefore He humbled their heart with labor; They stumbled and there was none to help.
NASB.13: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them out of their distresses.
NASB.14: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death And broke their bands apart.
NASB.15: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
NASB.16: For He has shattered gates of bronze And cut bars of iron asunder.
NASB.17: Fools, because of their rebellious way, And because of their iniquities, were afflicted.
NASB.18: Their soul abhorred all kinds of food, And they drew near to the gates of death.
NASB.19: Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble; He saved them out of their distresses.
NASB.20: He sent His word and healed them, And delivered [them] from their destructions.
NASB.21: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
NASB.22: Let them also offer sacrifices of thanksgiving, And tell of His works with joyful singing.
NASB.23: Those who go down to the sea in ships, Who do business on great waters;
NASB.24: They have seen the works of the LORD, And His wonders in the deep.
NASB.25: For He spoke and raised up a stormy wind, Which lifted up the waves of the sea.
NASB.26: They rose up to the heavens, they went down to the depths; Their soul melted away in [their] misery.
NASB.27: They reeled and staggered like a drunken man, And were at their wits' end.
NASB.28: Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble, And He brought them out of their distresses.
NASB.29: He caused the storm to be still, So that the waves of the sea were hushed.
NASB.30: Then they were glad because they were quiet, So He guided them to their desired haven.
NASB.31: Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness, And for His wonders to the sons of men!
NASB.32: Let them extol Him also in the congregation of the people, And praise Him at the seat of the elders.
NASB.33: He changes rivers into a wilderness And springs of water into a thirsty ground;
NASB.34: A fruitful land into a salt waste, Because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
NASB.35: He changes a wilderness into a pool of water And a dry land into springs of water;
NASB.36: And there He makes the hungry to dwell, So that they may establish an inhabited city,
NASB.37: And sow fields and plant vineyards, And gather a fruitful harvest.
NASB.38: Also He blesses them and they multiply greatly, And He does not let their cattle decrease.
NASB.39: When they are diminished and bowed down Through oppression, misery and sorrow,
NASB.40: He pours contempt upon princes And makes them wander in a pathless waste.
NASB.41: But He sets the needy securely on high away from affliction, And makes [his] families like a flock.
NASB.42: The upright see it and are glad; But all unrighteousness shuts its mouth.
NASB.43: Who is wise? Let him give heed to these things, And consider the lovingkindnesses of the LORD.

NLT.1:
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good!
His faithful love endures forever.
NLT.2:
Has the LORD redeemed you? Then speak out!
Tell others he has redeemed you from your enemies.
NLT.3:
For he has gathered the exiles from many lands,
from east and west,
from north and south.
NLT.4:
Some wandered in the wilderness,
lost and homeless.
NLT.6:
“LORD, help!” they cried in their trouble,
and he rescued them from their distress.
NLT.7:
He led them straight to safety,
to a city where they could live.
NLT.8:
Let them praise the LORD for his great love
and for the wonderful things he has done for them.
NLT.9:
For he satisfies the thirsty
and fills the hungry with good things.
NLT.10:
Some sat in darkness and deepest gloom,
imprisoned in iron chains of misery.
NLT.11:
They rebelled against the words of God,
scorning the counsel of the Most High.
NLT.12:
That is why he broke them with hard labor;
they fell, and no one was there to help them.
NLT.13:
“LORD, help!” they cried in their trouble,
and he saved them from their distress.
NLT.14:
He led them from the darkness and deepest gloom;
he snapped their chains.
NLT.15:
Let them praise the LORD for his great love
and for the wonderful things he has done for them.
NLT.16:
For he broke down their prison gates of bronze;
he cut apart their bars of iron.
NLT.17:
Some were fools; they rebelled
and suffered for their sins.
NLT.18:
They couldn’t stand the thought of food,
and they were knocking on death’s door.
NLT.19:
“LORD, help!” they cried in their trouble,
and he saved them from their distress.
NLT.20:
He sent out his word and healed them,
snatching them from the door of death.
NLT.21:
Let them praise the LORD for his great love
and for the wonderful things he has done for them.
NLT.22:
Let them offer sacrifices of thanksgiving
and sing joyfully about his glorious acts.
NLT.23:
Some went off to sea in ships,
plying the trade routes of the world.
NLT.24:
They, too, observed the LORD’s power in action,
his impressive works on the deepest seas.
NLT.25:
He spoke, and the winds rose,
stirring up the waves.
NLT.26:
Their ships were tossed to the heavens
and plunged again to the depths;
the sailors cringed in terror.
NLT.27:
They reeled and staggered like drunkards
and were at their wits’ end.
NLT.28:
“LORD, help!” they cried in their trouble,
and he saved them from their distress.
NLT.29:
He calmed the storm to a whisper
and stilled the waves.
NLT.30:
What a blessing was that stillness
as he brought them safely into harbor!
NLT.31:
Let them praise the LORD for his great love
and for the wonderful things he has done for them.
NLT.32:
Let them exalt him publicly before the congregation
and before the leaders of the nation.
NLT.33:
He changes rivers into deserts,
and springs of water into dry, thirsty land.
NLT.34:
He turns the fruitful land into salty wastelands,
because of the wickedness of those who live there.
NLT.35:
But he also turns deserts into pools of water,
the dry land into springs of water.
NLT.36:
He brings the hungry to settle there
and to build their cities.
NLT.37:
They sow their fields, plant their vineyards,
and harvest their bumper crops.
NLT.38:
How he blesses them!
They raise large families there,
and their herds of livestock increase.
NLT.39:
When they decrease in number and become impoverished
through oppression, trouble, and sorrow,
NLT.40:
the LORD pours contempt on their princes,
causing them to wander in trackless wastelands.
NLT.41:
But he rescues the poor from trouble
and increases their families like flocks of sheep.
NLT.42:
The godly will see these things and be glad,
while the wicked are struck silent.
NLT.43:
Those who are wise will take all this to heart;
they will see in our history the faithful love of the LORD.

WEB.1: Give thanks to Yahweh,  for he is good, for his loving kindness endures forever.
WEB.2: Let the redeemed by Yahweh say so, whom he has redeemed from the hand of the adversary,
WEB.3: And gathered out of the lands, from the east and from the west, from the north and from the south.
WEB.4: They wandered in the wilderness in a desert way. They found no city to live in.
WEB.5: Hungry and thirsty, their soul fainted in them.
WEB.6: Then they cried to Yahweh in their trouble, and he delivered them out of their distresses,
WEB.7: he led them also by a straight way, that they might go to a city to live in.
WEB.8: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds to the children of men!
WEB.9: For he satisfies the longing soul. He fills the hungry soul with good.
WEB.10: Some sat in darkness and in the shadow of death, being bound in affliction and iron,
WEB.11: because they rebelled against the words of God,  and condemned the counsel of the Most High.
WEB.12: Therefore he brought down their heart with labor. They fell down, and there was no one to help.
WEB.13: Then they cried to Yahweh in their trouble, and he saved them out of their distresses.
WEB.14: He brought them out of darkness and the shadow of death, and broke away their chains.
WEB.15: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds to the children of men!
WEB.16: For he has broken the gates of brass, and cut through bars of iron.
WEB.17: Fools are afflicted because of their disobedience, and because of their iniquities.
WEB.18: Their soul abhors all kinds of food. They draw near to the gates of death.
WEB.19: Then they cry to Yahweh in their trouble, he saves them out of their distresses.
WEB.20: He sends his word, and heals them, and delivers them from their graves.
WEB.21: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds to the children of men!
WEB.22: Let them offer the sacrifices of thanksgiving, and declare his deeds with singing.
WEB.23: Those who go down to the sea in ships, who do business in great waters;
WEB.24: These see Yahweh’s deeds, and his wonders in the deep.
WEB.25: For he commands, and raises the stormy wind, which lifts up its waves.
WEB.26: They mount up to the sky; they go down again to the depths. Their soul melts away because of trouble.
WEB.27: They reel back and forth, and stagger like a drunken man, and are at their wits’ end.
WEB.28: Then they cry to Yahweh in their trouble, and he brings them out of their distress.
WEB.29: He makes the storm a calm, so that its waves are still.
WEB.30: Then they are glad because it is calm, so he brings them to their desired haven.
WEB.31: Let them praise Yahweh for his loving kindness, for his wonderful deeds for the children of men!
WEB.32: Let them exalt him also in the assembly of the people, and praise him in the seat of the elders.
WEB.33: He turns rivers into a desert, water springs into a thirsty ground,
WEB.34: and a fruitful land into a salt waste, for the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
WEB.35: He turns a desert into a pool of water, and a dry land into water springs.
WEB.36: There he makes the hungry live, that they may prepare a city to live in,
WEB.37: sow fields, plant vineyards, and reap the fruits of increase.
WEB.38: He blesses them also, so that they are multiplied greatly. He doesn’t allow their livestock to decrease.
WEB.39: Again, they are diminished and bowed down through oppression, trouble, and sorrow.
WEB.40: He pours contempt on princes, and causes them to wander in a trackless waste.
WEB.41: Yet he lifts the needy out of their affliction, and increases their families like a flock.
WEB.42: The upright will see it, and be glad. All the wicked will shut their mouths.
WEB.43: Whoever is wise will pay attention to these things. They will consider the loving kindnesses of Yahweh.

CSB17.1:

his faithful love endures forever. ⓜ

that he has redeemed them from the power of the foe ⓝ

and has gathered them from the lands —

He broke their spirits [4] with hard labor;
they stumbled, and there was no one to help. ⓙ

Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble;
he saved them from their distress. ⓚ

He brought them out of darkness and gloom

Let them give thanks to the LORD
for his faithful love
and his wondrous works for all humanity. ⓜ
CSB17.16:
For he has broken down the bronze gates

CSB17.17:
Fools suffered affliction
because of their rebellious ways and their iniquities. ⓐ
CSB17.18:

and came near the gates of death. ⓑ
CSB17.19:
Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble;
he saved them from their distress. ⓒ

CSB17.21:
Let them give thanks to the LORD
for his faithful love
and his wondrous works for all humanity. ⓔ

and announce his works with shouts of joy. ⓕ
CSB17.23:
Others went to sea in ships,
conducting trade on the vast water. ⓖ
CSB17.24:

his wondrous works in the deep. ⓗ
CSB17.25:
He spoke and raised a stormy wind
that stirred up the waves of the sea. [5] ⓘ
CSB17.26:
Rising up to the sky, sinking down to the depths,
their courage [6] melting away in anguish, ⓙ
CSB17.27:
they reeled and staggered like a drunkard,
and all their skill was useless. ⓚ
CSB17.28:
Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble,
and he brought them out of their distress. ⓛ

He stilled the storm to a whisper,
and the waves of the sea [7] were hushed. ⓜ
CSB17.30:
They rejoiced when the waves [8] grew quiet.
Then he guided them to the harbor they longed for. ⓝ
CSB17.31:
Let them give thanks to the LORD
for his faithful love
and his wondrous works for all humanity. ⓐ
CSB17.32:
Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people
and praise him in the council of the elders. ⓑ
CSB17.33:
He turns rivers into desert,
springs into thirsty ground, ⓒ
CSB17.34:
and fruitful land into salty wasteland,
because of the wickedness of its inhabitants. ⓓ
CSB17.35:
He turns a desert into a pool,
dry land into springs. ⓔ
CSB17.36:
He causes the hungry to settle there,
and they establish a city where they can live. ⓕ
CSB17.37:
They sow fields and plant vineyards
that yield a fruitful harvest. ⓖ
CSB17.38:
He blesses them, and they multiply greatly; ⓗ
he does not let their livestock decrease. ⓘ
CSB17.39:
When they are diminished and are humbled
by cruel oppression and sorrow, ⓙ
CSB17.40:
he pours contempt on nobles ⓚ
and makes them wander in a trackless wasteland. ⓛ
CSB17.41:
But he lifts the needy out of their suffering ⓜ
and makes their families multiply like flocks. ⓝ
CSB17.42:
The upright see it and rejoice, ⓐ
and all injustice shuts its mouth. ⓑ
CSB17.43:
Let whoever is wise pay attention to these things
and consider the LORD’s acts of faithful love. ⓒ

AMP.1:
O give thanks to the LORD, for He is good;
For His compassion and lovingkindness endure forever!
AMP.2:
Let the redeemed of the LORD say so,
Whom He has redeemed from the hand of the adversary,
AMP.3:
And gathered them from the lands,
From the east and from the west,
From the north and from the south.
AMP.4:
They wandered in the wilderness in a [solitary] desert region;
And did not find a way to an inhabited city.
AMP.6:
Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble,
And He rescued them from their distresses.
AMP.7:
He led them by the straight way,
To an inhabited city [where they could establish their homes].
AMP.8:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness,
And for His wonderful acts to the children of men!
AMP.9:
For He satisfies the parched throat,
And fills the hungry appetite with what is good.
AMP.10:
Some dwelt in darkness and in the deep (deathly) darkness,
Prisoners [bound] in misery and chains,
AMP.11:
Because they had rebelled against the precepts of God
And spurned the counsel of the Most High.
AMP.12:
Therefore He humbled their heart with hard labor;
They stumbled and there was no one to help.
AMP.13:
Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble,
And He saved them from their distresses.
AMP.14:
He brought them out of darkness and the deep (deathly) darkness
And broke their bonds apart.
AMP.15:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness,
And for His wonderful acts to the children of men!
AMP.16:
For He has shattered the gates of bronze
And cut the bars of iron apart.
AMP.17:
Fools, because of their rebellious way,
And because of their sins, were afflicted.
AMP.18:
They detested all kinds of food,
And they drew near to the gates of death.
AMP.19:
Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble,
And He saved them from their distresses.
AMP.20:
He sent His word and healed them,
And rescued them from their destruction.
AMP.21:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness,
And for His wonderful acts to the children of men!
AMP.22:
And let them offer the sacrifices of thanksgiving,
And speak of His deeds with shouts of joy!
AMP.23:
Those who go down to the sea in ships,
Who do business on great waters;
AMP.24:
They have seen the works of the LORD,
And His wonders in the deep.
AMP.25:
For He spoke and raised up a stormy wind,
Which lifted up the waves of the sea.
AMP.26:
They went up toward the heavens [on the crest of the wave], they went down again to the depths [of the watery trough];
Their courage melted away in their misery.
AMP.27:
They staggered and trembled like a drunken man,
And were at their wits' end [all their wisdom was useless].
AMP.28:
Then they cried out to the LORD in their trouble,
And He brought them out of their distresses.
AMP.29:
He hushed the storm to a gentle whisper,
So that the waves of the sea were still.
AMP.30:
Then they were glad because of the calm,
And He guided them to their desired haven (harbor).
AMP.31:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for His lovingkindness,
And for His wonderful acts to the children of men!
AMP.32:
Let them exalt Him also in the congregation of the people,
And praise Him at the seat of the elders.
AMP.33:
He turns rivers into a wilderness,
And springs of water into a thirsty ground;
AMP.34:
A productive land into a [barren] salt waste,
Because of the wickedness of those who dwell in it.
AMP.35:
He turns a wilderness into a pool of water
And a dry land into springs of water;
AMP.36:
And there He has the hungry dwell,
So that they may establish an inhabited city,
AMP.37:
And sow fields and plant vineyards,
And produce an abundant harvest.
AMP.38:
Also He blesses them so that they multiply greatly,
And He does not let [the number of] their cattle decrease.
AMP.39:
When they are diminished and bowed down (humbled)
Through oppression, misery, and sorrow,
AMP.40:
He pours contempt on princes
And makes them wander in a pathless wasteland.
AMP.41:
Yet He sets the needy securely on high, away from affliction,
And makes their families like a flock.
AMP.42:
The upright see it and rejoice;
But all unrighteousness shuts its mouth.
AMP.43:
Who is wise? Let him observe and heed these things;
And [thoughtfully] consider the lovingkindness of the LORD.

MSG.1:
Oh, thank God—he's so good!
His love never runs out.
MSG.2:
All of you set free by God, tell the world!
Tell how he freed you from oppression,
MSG.3:
Then rounded you up from all over the place,
from the four winds, from the seven seas.
MSG.4:
Some of you wandered for years in the desert,
looking but not finding a good place to live,
MSG.5:
Half-starved and parched with thirst,
staggering and stumbling, on the brink of exhaustion.
MSG.6:
Then, in your desperate condition, you called out to God.
He got you out in the nick of time;
MSG.7:
He put your feet on a wonderful road
that took you straight to a good place to live.
MSG.8:
So thank God for his marvelous love,
for his miracle mercy to the children he loves.
MSG.9:
He poured great draughts of water down parched throats;
the starved and hungry got plenty to eat.
MSG.10:
Some of you were locked in a dark cell,
cruelly confined behind bars,
MSG.11:
Punished for defying God's Word,
for turning your back on the High God's counsel—
MSG.12:
A hard sentence, and your hearts so heavy,
and not a soul in sight to help.
MSG.13:
Then you called out to God in your desperate condition;
he got you out in the nick of time.
MSG.14:
He led you out of your dark, dark cell,
broke open the jail and led you out.
MSG.15:
So thank God for his marvelous love,
for his miracle mercy to the children he loves;
MSG.16:
He shattered the heavy jailhouse doors,
he snapped the prison bars like matchsticks!
MSG.17:
Some of you were sick because you'd lived a bad life,
your bodies feeling the effects of your sin;
MSG.18:
You couldn't stand the sight of food,
so miserable you thought you'd be better off dead.
MSG.19:
Then you called out to God in your desperate condition;
he got you out in the nick of time.
MSG.20:
He spoke the word that healed you,
that pulled you back from the brink of death.
MSG.21:
So thank God for his marvelous love,
for his miracle mercy to the children he loves;
MSG.22:
Offer thanksgiving sacrifices,
tell the world what he's done—sing it out!
MSG.23:
Some of you set sail in big ships;
you put to sea to do business in faraway ports.
MSG.24:
Out at sea you saw God in action,
saw his breathtaking ways with the ocean:
MSG.25:
With a word he called up the wind—
an ocean storm, towering waves!
MSG.26:
You shot high in the sky, then the bottom dropped out;
your hearts were stuck in your throats.
MSG.27:
You were spun like a top, you reeled like a drunk,
you didn't know which end was up.
MSG.28:
Then you called out to God in your desperate condition;
he got you out in the nick of time.
MSG.29:
He quieted the wind down to a whisper,
put a muzzle on all the big waves.
MSG.30:
And you were so glad when the storm died down,
and he led you safely back to harbor.
MSG.31:
So thank God for his marvelous love,
for his miracle mercy to the children he loves.
MSG.32:
Lift high your praises when the people assemble,
shout Hallelujah when the elders meet!
MSG.33:
God turned rivers into wasteland,
springs of water into sunbaked mud;
MSG.34:
Luscious orchards became alkali flats
because of the evil of the people who lived there.
MSG.35:
Then he changed wasteland into fresh pools of water,
arid earth into springs of water,
MSG.36:
Brought in the hungry and settled them there;
they moved in—what a great place to live!
MSG.37:
They sowed the fields, they planted vineyards,
they reaped a bountiful harvest.
MSG.38:
He blessed them and they prospered greatly;
their herds of cattle never decreased.
MSG.39: But abuse and evil and trouble declined
MSG.40:
as he heaped scorn on princes and sent them away.
He gave the poor a safe place to live,
MSG.41: treated their clans like well-cared-for sheep.
MSG.42:
Good people see this and are glad;
bad people are speechless, stopped in their tracks.
MSG.43:
If you are really wise, you'll think this over—
it's time you appreciated God's deep love.

NIV.1:
BOOK V
Psalms 107–150
Psalm 107
Give thanks to the Lord, for he is good;
his love endures forever.
NIV.2:
Let the redeemed of the Lord say this—
those he redeemed from the hand of the foe,
NIV.3:
those he gathered from the lands,
from east and west, from north and south.
NIV.4:
Some wandered in desert wastelands,
finding no way to a city where they could settle.
NIV.5:
They were hungry and thirsty,
and their lives ebbed away.
NIV.6:
Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble,
and he delivered them from their distress.
NIV.7:
He led them by a straight way
to a city where they could settle.
NIV.8:
Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love
and his wonderful deeds for men,
NIV.9:
for he satisfies the thirsty
and fills the hungry with good things.
NIV.10:
Some sat in darkness and the deepest gloom,
prisoners suffering in iron chains,
NIV.11:
for they had rebelled against the words of God
and despised the counsel of the Most High.
NIV.12:
So he subjected them to bitter labor;
they stumbled, and there was no one to help.
NIV.13:
Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble,
and he saved them from their distress.
NIV.14:
He brought them out of darkness and the deepest gloom
and broke away their chains.
NIV.15:
Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love
and his wonderful deeds for men,
NIV.16:
for he breaks down gates of bronze
and cuts through bars of iron.
NIV.17:
Some became fools through their rebellious ways
and suffered affliction because of their iniquities.
NIV.18:
They loathed all food
and drew near the gates of death.
NIV.19:
Then they cried to the Lord in their trouble,
and he saved them from their distress.
NIV.20:
He sent forth his word and healed them;
he rescued them from the grave.
NIV.21:
Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love
and his wonderful deeds for men.
NIV.22:
Let them sacrifice thank offerings
and tell of his works with songs of joy.
NIV.23:
Others went out on the sea in ships;
they were merchants on the mighty waters.
NIV.24:
They saw the works of the Lord,
his wonderful deeds in the deep.
NIV.25:
For he spoke and stirred up a tempest
that lifted high the waves.
NIV.26:
They mounted up to the heavens and went down to the depths;
in their peril their courage melted away.
NIV.27:
They reeled and staggered like drunken men;
they were at their wits’ end.
NIV.28:
Then they cried out to the Lord in their trouble,
and he brought them out of their distress.
NIV.29:
He stilled the storm to a whisper;
the waves of the sea were hushed.
NIV.30:
They were glad when it grew calm,
and he guided them to their desired haven.
NIV.31:
Let them give thanks to the Lord for his unfailing love
and his wonderful deeds for men.
NIV.32:
Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people
and praise him in the council of the elders.
NIV.33:
He turned rivers into a desert,
flowing springs into thirsty ground,
NIV.34:
and fruitful land into a salt waste,
because of the wickedness of those who lived there.
NIV.35:
He turned the desert into pools of water
and the parched ground into flowing springs;
NIV.36:
there he brought the hungry to live,
and they founded a city where they could settle.
NIV.37:
They sowed fields and planted vineyards
that yielded a fruitful harvest;
NIV.38:
he blessed them, and their numbers greatly increased,
and he did not let their herds diminish.
NIV.39:
Then their numbers decreased, and they were humbled
by oppression, calamity and sorrow;
NIV.40:
he who pours contempt on nobles
made them wander in a trackless waste.
NIV.41:
But he lifted the needy out of their affliction
and increased their families like flocks.
NIV.42:
The upright see and rejoice,
but all the wicked shut their mouths.
NIV.43:
Whoever is wise, let him heed these things
and consider the great love of the Lord.

NRSVCE.1:
O give thanks to the LORD, for he is good;
for his steadfast love endures forever.
NRSVCE.2:
Let the redeemed of the LORD say so,
those he redeemed from trouble
NRSVCE.3:
and gathered in from the lands,
from the east and from the west,
from the north and from the south.
NRSVCE.4:
Some wandered in desert wastes,
finding no way to an inhabited town;
NRSVCE.5:
hungry and thirsty,
their soul fainted within them.
NRSVCE.6:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble,
and he delivered them from their distress;
NRSVCE.7:
he led them by a straight way,
until they reached an inhabited town.
NRSVCE.8:
Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love,
for his wonderful works to humankind.
NRSVCE.9:
For he satisfies the thirsty,
and the hungry he fills with good things.
NRSVCE.10:
Some sat in darkness and in gloom,
prisoners in misery and in irons,
NRSVCE.11:
for they had rebelled against the words of God,
and spurned the counsel of the Most High.
NRSVCE.12:
Their hearts were bowed down with hard labor;
they fell down, with no one to help.
NRSVCE.13:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble,
and he saved them from their distress;
NRSVCE.14:
he brought them out of darkness and gloom,
and broke their bonds asunder.
NRSVCE.15:
Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love,
for his wonderful works to humankind.
NRSVCE.16:
For he shatters the doors of bronze,
and cuts in two the bars of iron.
NRSVCE.17:
Some were sick through their sinful ways,
and because of their iniquities endured affliction;
NRSVCE.18:
they loathed any kind of food,
and they drew near to the gates of death.
NRSVCE.19:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble,
and he saved them from their distress;
NRSVCE.20:
he sent out his word and healed them,
and delivered them from destruction.
NRSVCE.21:
Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love,
for his wonderful works to humankind.
NRSVCE.22:
And let them offer thanksgiving sacrifices,
and tell of his deeds with songs of joy.
NRSVCE.23:
Some went down to the sea in ships,
doing business on the mighty waters;
NRSVCE.24:
they saw the deeds of the LORD,
his wondrous works in the deep.
NRSVCE.25:
For he commanded and raised the stormy wind,
which lifted up the waves of the sea.
NRSVCE.26:
They mounted up to heaven, they went down to the depths;
their courage melted away in their calamity;
NRSVCE.27:
they reeled and staggered like drunkards,
and were at their wits’ end.
NRSVCE.28:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble,
and he brought them out from their distress;
NRSVCE.29:
he made the storm be still,
and the waves of the sea were hushed.
NRSVCE.30:
Then they were glad because they had quiet,
and he brought them to their desired haven.
NRSVCE.31:
Let them thank the LORD for his steadfast love,
for his wonderful works to humankind.
NRSVCE.32:
Let them extol him in the congregation of the people,
and praise him in the assembly of the elders.
NRSVCE.33:
He turns rivers into a desert,
springs of water into thirsty ground,
NRSVCE.34:
a fruitful land into a salty waste,
because of the wickedness of its inhabitants.
NRSVCE.35:
He turns a desert into pools of water,
a parched land into springs of water.
NRSVCE.36:
And there he lets the hungry live,
and they establish a town to live in;
NRSVCE.37:
they sow fields, and plant vineyards,
and get a fruitful yield.
NRSVCE.38:
By his blessing they multiply greatly,
and he does not let their cattle decrease.
NRSVCE.39:
When they are diminished and brought low
through oppression, trouble, and sorrow,
NRSVCE.40:
he pours contempt on princes
and makes them wander in trackless wastes;
NRSVCE.41:
but he raises up the needy out of distress,
and makes their families like flocks.
NRSVCE.42:
The upright see it and are glad;
and all wickedness stops its mouth.
NRSVCE.43:
Let those who are wise give heed to these things,
and consider the steadfast love of the LORD.

NET.1: Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good, and his loyal love endures!
NET.2: Let those delivered by the LORD speak out, those whom he delivered from the power of the enemy,
NET.3: and gathered from foreign lands, from east and west, from north and south.
NET.4: They wandered through the wilderness on a desert road; they found no city in which to live.
NET.5: They were hungry and thirsty; they fainted from exhaustion.
NET.6: They cried out to the LORD in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
NET.7: He led them on a level road, that they might find a city in which to live.
NET.8: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people!
NET.9: For he has satisfied those who thirst, and those who hunger he has filled with food.
NET.10: They sat in utter darkness, bound in painful iron chains,
NET.11: because they had rebelled against God’s commands, and rejected the instructions of the sovereign king.
NET.12: So he used suffering to humble them; they stumbled and no one helped them up.
NET.13: They cried out to the LORD in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
NET.14: He brought them out of the utter darkness, and tore off their shackles.
NET.15: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people!
NET.16: For he shattered the bronze gates, and hacked through the iron bars.
NET.17: They acted like fools in their rebellious ways, and suffered because of their sins.
NET.18: They lost their appetite for all food, and they drew near the gates of death.
NET.19: They cried out to the LORD in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
NET.20: He sent them an assuring word and healed them; he rescued them from the pits where they were trapped.
NET.21: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people!
NET.22: Let them present thank offerings, and loudly proclaim what he has done!
NET.23: Some traveled on the sea in ships, and carried cargo over the vast waters.
NET.24: They witnessed the acts of the LORD, his amazing feats on the deep water.
NET.25: He gave the order for a windstorm, and it stirred up the waves of the sea.
NET.26: They reached up to the sky, then dropped into the depths. The sailors’ strength left them because the danger was so great.
NET.27: They swayed and staggered like a drunk, and all their skill proved ineffective.
NET.28: They cried out to the LORD in their distress; he delivered them from their troubles.
NET.29: He calmed the storm, and the waves grew silent.
NET.30: The sailors rejoiced because the waves grew quiet, and he led them to the harbor they desired.
NET.31: Let them give thanks to the LORD for his loyal love, and for the amazing things he has done for people!
NET.32: Let them exalt him in the assembly of the people! Let them praise him in the place where the leaders preside!
NET.33: He turned streams into a desert, springs of water into arid land,
NET.34: and a fruitful land into a barren place, because of the sin of its inhabitants.
NET.35: As for his people, he turned a desert into a pool of water, and a dry land into springs of water.
NET.36: He allowed the hungry to settle there, and they established a city in which to live.
NET.37: They cultivated fields, and planted vineyards, which yielded a harvest of fruit.
NET.38: He blessed them so that they became very numerous. He would not allow their cattle to decrease in number.
NET.39: As for their enemies, they decreased in number and were beaten down, because of painful distress and suffering.
NET.40: He would pour contempt upon princes, and he made them wander in a wasteland with no road.
NET.41: Yet he protected the needy from oppression, and cared for his families like a flock of sheep.
NET.42: When the godly see this, they rejoice, and every sinner shuts his mouth.
NET.43: Whoever is wise, let him take note of these things! Let them consider the LORD’s acts of loyal love!
